question: Looking at the US median income map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The median income gap between Oklahoma and Wyoming might be larger than that between North Carolina and West Virginia.
options.B: Oklahoma and Wyoming have similar median incomes.
options.C: All highlighted states have very similar median income levels.
options.D: The median income differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.

explanation: Oklahoma (83.1$k) and Wyoming (106.3$k) have a 23.2$k median income difference, while California (64.8$k) and Louisiana (69.6$k) have only a 4.8$k difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Oklahoma and Wyoming into the same color bin (65$k+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while California and Louisiana appear more different due to different color bins.
